At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.

We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.

Join EY and help to build a better working world.

Senior- FTS And V (Process/TOM)

EY – GDS – Consulting – Finance

As part of our EY - Finance Consulting Team, you will help clients develop their Performance Improvement initiatives and identify potential opportunities for performance improvements drive them. This includes techno-functional role conducting finance processes assessments, deep diving and analysing pain areas and coming up with solutions. The client base spans across industries and countries.

The opportunity

We’re looking for Consultant / Sr. Consultant with expertise in Process Assessment assignments with process mapping experience. Also, experience in FP&A, Financial Accounting and Reporting domain is an added advantage to join the global EY - Finance Consulting team. This is a fantastic opportunity to be part of a leading firm whilst being instrumental in the growth of a new service offering.

Your Key Responsibilities

• Experience with finance functions – ‘Record to report’, ‘Procure to pay’ and ‘Order to cash’ processes involving account reconciliations, Journals, Transaction matching, Variance analysis, intercompany and Fixed assets accounting / management
• Conduct workshops with clients to perform current state As-Is process assessment and design & optimize Future state processes as per leading practices & industry benchmarks
• Identify process improvement opportunities using automation, technology upgradation & process standardization, Finance Transformation and Operation model design and Assessments
• Create L1-L5 “As-Is” and “To-Be” process mapping using tools such as Planview, Nimbus, ARIS, IBM Blueworks and Visio
• Experience in implementation and development of process mapping tools for business functions
• Design KPIs and Control frameworks for O2C, P2P and R2R processes
• Strong accounting skills and understands financial reports and statements
• Knowledge on Financial Consolidation, Cost Allocation, Finance Planning and Budgeting Processes
• Should have knowledge of activities in financial close of an organisation for month / Quarter / Annual close periods
• Good Knowledge on ledger and sub-ledger systems; technical know-how of SAP ERP / HANA, Oracle GL (good to have)
• Translate business requirements to technical language and model (good to have)
• Good experience in Chart of accounts (CoA) design
• Managing/ supervising teams of people on project work
• Ability to effectively prioritize and execute tasks in a high-pressure
• Open to travel (Domestic & International)

Skills And Attributes For Success

• Consulting experience is a good to have
• Confident & professional communication style
• Proficiency in English (oral and written)
• Ability to clearly articulate messages to a variety of audiences
• Flexible and adaptable; able to work in ambiguous situations
• Able to work effectively at all levels in an organization
• Capability to influence others and move toward a common vision or goal
• Must be a team player and able to work collaboratively with and through others
• Strong analytical skills as well as excellent problem-solving skills for interpreting the IFRS standards

To qualify for the role, you must have

• CA, CFA, CPA, MBA OR Master’s in business administration in Finance
• 1-2 years (for Staff level) and 3-6 years (for Senior level) of work experience in Finance domain
• Strong Excel and PowerPoint skills

Ideally, you’ll also have

• Exposure to tools like ERP, HFM, Visio, ARIS, BlueWorks, Celonis, Alteryx, etc.
• Project management skills, stakeholder management
• Finance Transformation

What We Look For

• A Team of people with commercial acumen, technical experience and enthusiasm to learn new things in this fast-moving environment
• Opportunities to work with EY Advisory practices globally with leading businesses across a range of industries

What Working At EY Offers

At EY, we’re dedicated to helping our clients, from world’s top companies — and the work we do with them is as varied as they are. You get to work with inspiring and meaningful projects. Our focus is education and coaching alongside practical experience to ensure your personal development. We value our employees and you will be able to control your own development with an individual progression plan. You will quickly grow into a responsible role with challenging and stimulating assignments. Moreover, you will be part of an interdisciplinary environment that emphasizes high quality and knowledge exchange. Plus, we offer:

• Support, coaching and feedback from some of the most engaging colleagues around
• Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career
• The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you

EY | Building a better working world

EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.

Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.

EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.

The role is responsible for end-to-end tax compliance, accounting close support, and inter-company transaction management for APAC operations. The position ensures timely statutory compliance, accurate financial reporting, robust reconciliations, and audit readiness, while working closely with internal stakeholders, banks, and external consultants.

Responsibilities
• Finalize and file GSTR-1, GSTR-2, GSTR-3B, Annual Returns, and LUT within statutory timelines.
• Perform GST turnover reconciliation, handle GST payments, challan uploads, approvals, and utilization entries.
• Manage ISD returns, input reclassification entries, and GST TDS reconciliation.

• Coordinate for quarterly TDS return filings and processing advance tax payments.
• Perform inter-company reconciliations, knock-offs, and receivable tracking.

• Compiling and providing GST, TDS, advance tax, and inter-company data.

Qualifications
• Education: 1st Attempt or 2nd Attempt Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) only.
• Work Experience : 5-8 years with Indirect Taxation (GST), Direct Tax (TDS, Advance Tax), Intercompany Accounting.

• Must have: Experience working with Big4 / external consultant is preferred

**Selected intern's day-to-day responsibilities include**:
1. Handling the accounting entries in Tally Prime /other accounting software
2. Working on bookkeeping and auditing
3. Working on statutory audits, internal audits, etc.
4. Assisting in maintaining documentation and filings
5. Filing of income-tax, TDS, and GST returns
6. Working on ledger scrutiny and finalization of accounts
7. Working on the audit of various types of organizations
8. Handle company incorporation and ROC work

data.job_title: Director, Global Finance Centre of Excellence - CA
data.job_description:
About Company
Company operates in the digital entertainment services industry, providing creative and technical production support for interactive media and gaming projects.

Job Summary
This new role is critical to establishing and embedding a Global Centre of Excellence in the heart of the Finance organization. The Director, Global Finance Centre of Excellence will lead the implementation of the new team and be a highly visible advocate for the team and the services it delivers. The initial focus will be on standing up the team and managing the successful transition of Controlling activities from current locations. Thereafter, focus will shift to ensuring excellent service delivery, by developing a suite of operational KPIs to manage the performance of the Centre of Excellence team, developing clear channels of communication and strong relationships with the retained Finance teams and establishing Service Level Agreements and points of contact with Studio teams to ensure expectations on service delivery are clearly defined and monitored. A culture that drives continuous improvement will also be embedded. The Global Centre of Excellence is also expected to be fully involved in key projects, including Finance systems implementation and performance improvement initiatives to achieve best in class revenue processes and improve working capital. The Director, Finance Centre of Excellence will work alongside other Senior Finance team leaders and be a strong advocate for our Finance Excellence vision to be trusted business partners that champion growth, create value and manage risk.

Key Responsibilities
The Director, Global Finance Centre of Excellence will direct and develop the Centre of Excellence team and ensure delivery of the following key activities:

Centre of Excellence Set Up
• Work with Finance leadership to create the blueprint for the Global Centre of Excellence including scope of services, detailed team structure, hiring plans and transition management approach
• Create a resourcing model and detailed roles profiles to enable the hiring of team members
• Manage the hiring and onboarding of team members
• Ensure office infrastructure, systems access and other enablers are ready for when team members onboard
• Establish the working arrangements for the team including working hours, office/hybrid working, approach to team building and staff engagement
• Manage the transition of work to the Centre of Excellence, ensuring that process guides and work instructions are documented and that comprehensive readiness assessments are performed before ownership of any work transitions to the Centre of Excellence
• Manage the post transition Hypercare period ensuring that any issues around service delivery are quickly remediated
• Take part in activities to communicate the Centre of Excellence purpose, operation and team ensuring that users are crystal clear on how to engage with the team

Service Delivery
• Maintain a detailed and up to date record of all in scope activities ‘service catalogue’ being delivered and the key stakeholders to refer to for support service delivery and to handoff deliverables
• Operate a resource plan to ensure that staff are available to perform activities according to the agreed timetable including at peak times and during holiday periods
• Deliver services and maintain a set of operational KPIs that can track timelines, cost and accuracy of service delivery
• Develop escalation paths with the retained Finance teams so it is clear who should be contacted in the Centre of Excellence team to help resolve queries
• Work with the teams in the Studios to develop Service Level Agreements that set out clear expectations on service delivery and turnaround times for managing queries and exceptions
• Put in place periodic management / peer reviews to test that services are being delivered according to agreed processes and work instruction
• As directed, participate in activities to support external and internal audit
Working with the Group Financial Controlling and other Finance Teams
• Partner with the Directors, Regional Controlling to review performance and identify opportunities to improve service delivery
• Take part in activities with the Regional Controller’s team to build the Centre of Excellence team’s understanding of key business drivers, resolve issues and jointly develop opportunities for process improvement
• Work with the Group Financial Controller’s team, including Tax and Finance Systems to leverage existing know-how around process and systems

Controlling Projects and Initiatives
• Participate in projects sponsored by HQ Controlling to drive growth and performance improvement, including process standardization initiatives and systems implementation
• Create a culture continuous improvement

Finance Team Management and Collaboration
• Manage the Centre of Excellence team, setting goals for the Pillar Managers that cascade to their teams
• Create plans for the personal development of team members, including succession plans and performance improvement plans
• Ensure the Centre of Excellence team receive regular communication from Finance Leadership to help them become an integral and valued part of the Finance function
• Work as key member of the Finance leadership team, helping to shape, advocate for and deliver our Finance Excellence vision to be trusted business partners that champion growth, create value and manage risk

Job Requirements
This is an important new role in the organization that requires a strong, experienced and credible leader, with excellent understanding of all Controlling processes, ability to manage large teams, manage relationships and positively influence change. To be successful in the role it is expected that you will be:
• Qualified Chartered Accountant with 15 yrs experience working as a senior leader within a Global Finance function
• Have strong understanding of Controlling processes, controls and accounting policies
• Experience of creating a captive Centre of Excellence from the ground up, recruiting a high performance team and managing the transition of activities from a diverse set of locations
• Ability to manage service delivery though KPIs and develop and manage to Service Level Agreements agreed with the Studios
• Be able to trouble shoot and remediate accounting problems at source
• Confidence when leading process and systems change and ensuring the adoption of new ways of working'
• Be proactive and able to manage successful relations with Finance team members, including onshore teams and external bodies including external auditors and tax authorities
• Demonstrable track record of financial leadership, managing change and building and motivating high-performing teams
• Excellent team leadership skills and collaborative and inclusive approach to working with team members and colleagues

Hiring DGM-Accounting COE- IFRS 9/IFRS 15/ IFRS 16 with VOIS (Vodafone Intelligent Solutions)

About Job

Experience level: 8-16years

Location: Pune, Hybrid

Primary Skillset: Implementation experience in IFRS 9/IFRS 15/ IFRS

Qualification Required: CA/CMA/MBA in Finance

Job Description:

About this Role

The DGM – Accounting Centre of Excellence (IFRS 9 / IFRS 15 / IFRS 16) is a pivotal role within Vodafone’s Accounting Competence Centre, operating under the Group’s End‑to‑End Accounting & Reporting framework.

The individual provides strategic accounting leadership, ensures compliance with IFRS standards, and acts as a central governance authority supporting Vodafone’s markets globally.

As part of Lean Corporate Services (LCS), the Accounting CoE is responsible for driving standardisation, automation and high‑quality reporting across Vodafone Group. The role contributes directly to shaping, governing and continually improving global Record‑to‑Report (RTR) processes.

Who are you

You are a technically strong and collaborative accounting professional with deep expertise in IFRS 9, IFRS 15 and IFRS 16. You enjoy working in complex, cross‑functional environments and excel at guiding stakeholders through accounting judgement, governance and assurance.

You combine analytical skills with strong communication abilities, and you take pride in enabling high‑quality, compliant and timely financial reporting across multiple markets.

What you will do

Accounting Centre of Excellence & Governance
• Provide functional guidance and ensure consistent adherence to IFRS 9, IFRS 15 and IFRS 16 across markets.
• Lead accounting projects with detailed understanding of Vodafone‑specific commercial scenarios, contractual arrangements and accounting outcomes.
• Collaborate with Group Financial Reporting and Accounting Principles & Advisory teams to ensure alignment of policy, judgement and application.
• Support local Chief Accountants in delivering accurate financial statements, disclosures and management reporting under IFRS or local GAAP.
• Partner with RTR solutions and transformation teams to drive strategic accounting initiatives globally.
• Provide expertise for major commercial, transformation and VOIS process improvement projects.
• Strengthen and evolve global RTR frameworks, including Balance Sheet Reconciliations, Revenue, Lease and Accrual Governance.

Key Accountabilities
• Act as the CoE subject‑matter specialist for IFRS 9 / IFRS 15 / IFRS 16 across cross‑functional programmes.
• Own accounting considerations from design to implementation for transformation initiatives.
• Propose efficient, compliant and scalable accounting solutions tailored to business needs.
• Translate business requirements into actionable accounting and reporting outcomes.
• Maintain oversight of process performance, quality gaps and opportunities for continuous improvement.
• Drive automation, standardisation and control enhancements across IFRS processes.
• Maintain strong governance frameworks supporting accurate and compliant reporting.

What skills you need

Core Competencies, Knowledge & Experience
• Expert understanding of IFRS 9, IFRS 15 and IFRS 16, including application to complex commercial and financial structures.
• Comprehensive knowledge of revenue recognition, financial instruments and lease accounting.
• Experience in large, dynamic and matrixed organisations.
• Strong technical accounting judgement and problem‑solving skills.
• Excellent communication abilities to explain complex concepts clearly.
• A collaborative and customer‑focused mindset.

Must‑Have Technical / Professional Qualifications
• Chartered Accountant (or equivalent).
• Minimum 3 years’ hands‑on experience with IFRS 9 / IFRS 15 / IFRS 16.
• Demonstrated experience in finance transformation projects.
• Strong IT and data analytics literacy.
• Understanding of the telecommunications industry.
• Skilled in coordinating stakeholders and delivering high‑quality outcomes.
• Analytical, adaptable and collaborative approach with strong prioritisation abilities.

Key Performance Indicators
• Clean audit results with no material issues.
• On‑time and high‑quality Income Statement and Balance Sheet Reviews across markets.
• Strong governance and effective collaboration with internal and external stakeholders.

What skills you will learn
• Advanced application of IFRS across diverse commercial scenarios.
• Exposure to large‑scale global transformation programmes.
• Enhanced data and automation literacy within finance processes.
• Broader strategic understanding of Vodafone’s global markets and governance frameworks.
• Cross‑functional leadership in a complex, multinational environment.

About VOIS

VOIS (Vodafone Intelligent Solutions) is a strategic arm of Vodafone Group Plc, creating value and enhancing quality and efficiency across 28 countries, and operating from 7 locations: Albania, Egypt, Hungary, India, Romania, Spain and the UK.

Over 29,000 highly skilled individuals are dedicated to being Vodafone Group’s partner of choice for talent, technology, and transformation. We deliver the best services across IT, Business Intelligence Services, Customer Operations, Business Operations, HR, Finance, Supply Chain, HR Operations, and many more.

Established in 2006, VOIS has evolved into a global, multi-functional organisation, a Centre of Excellence for Intelligent Solutions focused on adding value and delivering business outcomes for Vodafone.

#VOIS #BeUnrivalled #CreateTheFuture

About Vodafone

Vodafone is a leading telecommunications company across Europe and Africa, continuously investing in advanced technologies to improve lives globally. As part of the Vodafone family—whether Vodafone, Vodacom, or VOIS—you will contribute to a culture of innovation, inclusion, and purpose. We value equality of opportunity and celebrate diversity in all forms. We maintain a workplace free from discrimination based on race, colour, age, gender identification, sexual orientation, disability, religion, nationality, and more.

About the Role:

The Supply Chain Risk Analyst will be responsible for identifying, assessing, and managing risks associated with third parties. The analyst will work closely with various departments and stakeholders to develop and implement effective risk management strategies. Additionally, the analyst will be responsible for understanding and mitigating Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) risks within the supply chain. This includes evaluating the environmental impact of suppliers, ensuring fair labor practices, and maintaining compliance with governance standards.

Responsibilities:
• Risk Assessment: Assess risks including Geographical, Reputational, Cyber Security, Sanctions, Human Resources, Business Continuity, Anti-Bribery/Anti-Corruption, Artificial Intelligence, ESG, and Privacy.
• Daily Operations: Perform day-to-day responsibilities of the SCRM program, including intake and handling of requests for new third parties, gathering documentation for due diligence and onboarding tasks, and maintaining the SCRM database.
• Monitoring: Monitor third parties for changes in their risk profile, including mergers and acquisitions, regulatory changes, and other external factors.
• Risk Assessment: Conduct risk assessments on new and existing third parties.
• Database Maintenance: Ensure the integrity of the SCRM database by accurately documenting and consistently updating contact information, products, and services to maintain reliable and trustworthy data.
• Reporting: Assist in generating standardized reports to inform business owners and management on third-party risk and performance.
• Risk Management: Encourage and enable effective risk management change.
• Support: Provide support and expertise to business owners on third-party relationship management.
• Incident Management: Conduct ongoing management of escalations and incidents related to due diligence and monitoring of third parties.
• Remediation: Facilitate or assist in the remediation of any third-party-related findings or issues.
• Program Improvement: Contribute to the improvement of the Supply Chain Risk Management Program.
• Vendor Communication: Communicate with third-party vendors to address identified risks and ensure compliance with contractual obligations and regulatory requirements.
• Industry Trends: Stay abreast of industry trends and best practices related to Supply Chain Risk Management.
• Special Projects: Assist with any special projects as needed.
• Bachelor’s degree in Information Security, Business Administration, or a related field.
• Minimum of 2 years of experience in third-party risk

Our Interview Practices

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.
